quoted.text:
BREAKING: Iran's IRGC announces it launched ballistic missiles and drones at eight US military targets, striking the US Fifth Fleet at Salman Port in Bahrain and the Ali Al Salem Base in Kuwait, "destroying them and decisively responding to recent US aggressions," per Tasnim.

The IRGC declares that the US strikes "violated Article 1 of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding" and "will result in a total halt of all processes."

The statement adds that from now on "violating vessels will be dealt with more strongly than in the past" and "will face a crushing response."

text:
➡️ Follow-up -- President Putin himself spoke at today's meeting on the fuel crisis. 
➡️ None of his comments were ground-breaking, but rather seemed calculated to show that the issue is being addressed at the highest level. 
#oott #Russia https://t.co/IpWerO9ixQ

quoted.text:
➡️ Three headlines today regarding the Russian fuel crisis, all from Deputy PM Alexander Novak:
1⃣  Gasoline imports are one possible measure to be taken. 
2⃣ The government will again take up the idea of a ban on diesel exports on Monday, and will be considered "on an ongoing basis".
3⃣ The minimum requirement for gasoline sales on the SPIMEX wholesale market has been reduced from 15% of output to 10%. 
➡️ Context and thoughts: 
  ✳️ Gasoline imports have been mooted before, and at this point seem to be a foregone conclusion (Russia currently levies zero export duty on gasoline imports). 
  ✳️ The diesel ban probably will happen, but be temporary and triggered by the start of harvest sometime in July. 
  ✳️ The reduction in SPIMEX volume requirements is likely something of a compromise: The big refiners need to supply their own retail chains first before making volumes available to SPIMEX, but there's currently not enough to go around, and the independent retailers are dependent upon the wholesale market.  
#oott #Russia
quoted.author: RusOilGasExpert

quoted.text:
Burnham is reportedly reconsidering Miliband as Chancellor over one specific reason: Miliband refuses to budge on the North Sea oil and gas ban even as the Iran war drives fuel prices through the roof. 

Think about what that tells us. 

The man being put forward to run the nation's finances holds an energy policy position so extreme that even the person appointing him thinks it is economically dangerous. Britain has some of the highest electricity costs in the developed world. 

The National Energy System Operator's own report found that slowing Miliband's net zero timeline could save the economy £350 billion. 

A Chancellor who refuses to acknowledge that basic trade-off is not a reformer — he is an ideologue with a chequebook.
quoted.author: stuey_beef

quoted.text:
I was bullish on oil long before Hormuz got closed.

- US shale provided ~90% of oil production growth over the last decade
- Shale is peaking
- We have massively underinvested in oil exploration
- It is becoming harder to find new sources of oil
- Global decline rates are accelerating

In cyclical industries, the rule of thumb is: buy in periods of sustained underinvestment, sell when the sector has clearly overinvested.

By any reasonable measure, oil is still in the underinvestment part of that cycle.
quoted.author: ekwufinance
